classes.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.media_type.MediaType

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.media_type.MediaType.media_type_schema
documentation: The schema defining the content of the request, response, or parameter.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.media_type.MediaType.example
documentation:
Example of the media type.

The example object SHOULD be in the correct format as specified by the media type.

The `example` field is mutually exclusive of the `examples` field.

Furthermore, if referencing a `schema` which contains an example,
the `example` value SHALL _override_ the example provided by the schema.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.media_type.MediaType.examples
documentation:
Examples of the media type.

Each example object SHOULD match the media type and specified schema if present.

The `examples` field is mutually exclusive of the `example` field.

Furthermore, if referencing a `schema` which contains an example,
the `examples` value SHALL _override_ the example provided by the schema.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.media_type.MediaType.encoding
documentation:
A map between a property name and its encoding information.
The key, being the property name, MUST exist in the schema as a property.
The encoding object SHALL only apply to `requestBody` objects
when the media type is `multipart` or `application/x-www-form-urlencoded`.
Each Media Type Object provides schema and examples for the media type identified by its key.

classes.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.server.Server

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.server.Server.url
documentation:
**REQUIRED**. A URL to the target host.

This URL supports Server Variables and MAY be relative,
to indicate that the host location is relative to the location where the OpenAPI document is being served.
Variable substitutions will be made when a variable is named in `{`brackets`}`.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.server.Server.description
documentation:
An optional string describing the host designated by the URL.
[CommonMark syntax](https://spec.commonmark.org/) MAY be used for rich text representation.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.server.Server.variables
documentation:
A map between a variable name and its value.

The value is used for substitution in the server's URL template.
An object representing a Server.

classes.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.request_body.RequestBody

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.request_body.RequestBody.description
documentation:
A brief description of the request body.
This could contain examples of use.  

[CommonMark syntax](https://spec.commonmark.org/) MAY be used for rich text representation.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.request_body.RequestBody.content
documentation:
**REQUIRED**. The content of the request body.
The key is a media type or [media type range](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#appendix-D)
and the value describes it.

For requests that match multiple keys, only the most specific key is applicable. e.g. text/plain overrides text/*

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.request_body.RequestBody.required
documentation: Determines if the request body is required in the request. Defaults to `false`.
Describes a single request body.

classes.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.xml.XML

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.xml.XML.name
documentation:
Replaces the name of the element/attribute used for the described schema property.
When defined within `items`, it will affect the name of the individual XML elements within the list.
When defined alongside `type` being `array` (outside the `items`),
it will affect the wrapping element and only if `wrapped` is `true`.
If `wrapped` is `false`, it will be ignored.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.xml.XML.namespace
documentation:
The URI of the namespace definition.
Value MUST be in the form of an absolute URI.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.xml.XML.prefix
documentation: The prefix to be used for the [name](#xmlName).

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.xml.XML.attribute
documentation:
Declares whether the property definition translates to an attribute instead of an element.
Default value is `false`.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.xml.XML.wrapped
documentation:
MAY be used only for an array definition.
Signifies whether the array is wrapped (for example, `<books><book/><book/></books>`)
or unwrapped (`<book/><book/>`).
Default value is `false`.
The definition takes effect only when defined alongside `type` being `array` (outside the `items`).

A metadata object that allows for more fine-tuned XML model definitions.

When using arrays, XML element names are *not* inferred (for singular/plural forms)
and the `name` property SHOULD be used to add that information.
See examples for expected behavior.

classes.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.operation.Operation

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.operation.Operation.tags
documentation:
A list of tags for API documentation control.
Tags can be used for logical grouping of operations by resources or any other qualifier.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.operation.Operation.summary
documentation: A short summary of what the operation does.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.operation.Operation.description
documentation:
A verbose explanation of the operation behavior.
[CommonMark syntax](https://spec.commonmark.org/) MAY be used for rich text representation.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.operation.Operation.externalDocs
documentation: Additional external documentation for this operation.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.operation.Operation.operationId
documentation:
Unique string used to identify the operation.
The id MUST be unique among all operations described in the API.
The operationId value is **case-sensitive**.
Tools and libraries MAY use the operationId to uniquely identify an operation,
therefore, it is RECOMMENDED to follow common programming naming conventions.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.operation.Operation.parameters
documentation:
A list of parameters that are applicable for this operation.
If a parameter is already defined at the [Path Item](#pathItemParameters),
the new definition will override it but can never remove it.
The list MUST NOT include duplicated parameters.
A unique parameter is defined by a combination of a [name](#parameterName) and [location](#parameterIn).
The list can use the [Reference Object](#referenceObject) to link to parameters
that are defined at the [OpenAPI Object's components/parameters](#componentsParameters).

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.operation.Operation.requestBody
documentation:
The request body applicable for this operation.  

The `requestBody` is only supported in HTTP methods where the HTTP 1.1 specification
[RFC7231](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-4.3.1) has explicitly defined semantics for request bodies. 
In other cases where the HTTP spec is vague, `requestBody` SHALL be ignored by consumers.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.operation.Operation.responses
documentation: **REQUIRED**. The list of possible responses as they are returned from executing this operation.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.operation.Operation.callbacks
documentation:
A map of possible out-of band callbacks related to the parent operation.
The key is a unique identifier for the Callback Object.
Each value in the map is a [Callback Object](#callbackObject) 
that describes a request that may be initiated by the API provider and the expected responses.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.operation.Operation.deprecated
documentation:
Declares this operation to be deprecated.
Consumers SHOULD refrain from usage of the declared operation.
Default value is `false`.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.operation.Operation.security
documentation:
A declaration of which security mechanisms can be used for this operation.
The list of values includes alternative security requirement objects that can be used.
Only one of the security requirement objects need to be satisfied to authorize a request.
To make security optional, an empty security requirement (`{}`) can be included in the array.
This definition overrides any declared top-level [`security`](#oasSecurity).
To remove a top-level security declaration, an empty array can be used.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.operation.Operation.servers
documentation:
An alternative `server` array to service this operation.
If an alternative `server` object is specified at the Path Item Object or Root level, 
it will be overridden by this value.
Describes a single API operation on a path.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.example.Example.summary
documentation: Short description for the example.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.example.Example.description
documentation:
Long description for the example.
[CommonMark syntax](https://spec.commonmark.org/) MAY be used for rich text representation.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.example.Example.value
documentation:
Embedded literal example.
The `value` field and `externalValue` field are mutually exclusive.
To represent examples of media types that cannot naturally represented in JSON or YAML,
use a string value to contain the example, escaping where necessary.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.example.Example.externalValue
documentation:
A URL that points to the literal example.
This provides the capability to reference examples that cannot easily be included in JSON or YAML documents.

The `value` field and `externalValue` field are mutually exclusive.

classes.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.open_api.OpenAPI

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.open_api.OpenAPI.openapi
documentation:
**REQUIRED**. This string MUST be the [semantic version number](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html)
of the [OpenAPI Specification version](#versions) that the OpenAPI document uses. 
The `openapi` field SHOULD be used by tooling specifications and clients to interpret the OpenAPI document. 
This is *not* related to the API [`info.version`](#infoVersion) string.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.open_api.OpenAPI.info
documentation: **REQUIRED**. Provides metadata about the API. The metadata MAY be used by tooling as required.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.open_api.OpenAPI.servers
documentation:
An array of Server Objects, which provide connectivity information to a target server. 
If the `servers` property is not provided, or is an empty array,
the default value would be a [Server Object](#serverObject) with a [url](#serverUrl) value of `/`.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.open_api.OpenAPI.paths
documentation: **REQUIRED**. The available paths and operations for the API.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.open_api.OpenAPI.components
documentation: An element to hold various schemas for the specification.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.open_api.OpenAPI.security
documentation:
A declaration of which security mechanisms can be used across the API. 
The list of values includes alternative security requirement objects that can be used. 
Only one of the security requirement objects need to be satisfied to authorize a request.  
Individual operations can override this definition. 
To make security optional, an empty security requirement (`{}`) can be included in the array.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.open_api.OpenAPI.tags
documentation:
A list of tags used by the specification with additional metadata.
The order of the tags can be used to reflect on their order by the parsing tools.
Not all tags that are used by the [Operation Object](#operationObject) must be declared.
The tags that are not declared MAY be organized randomly or based on the tools' logic.
Each tag name in the list MUST be unique.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.open_api.OpenAPI.externalDocs
documentation: Additional external documentation.
This is the root document object of the OpenAPI document.

classes.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.components.Components

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.components.Components.schemas
documentation: An object to hold reusable [Schema Objects](#schemaObject).

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.components.Components.responses
documentation: An object to hold reusable [Response Objects](#responseObject).

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.components.Components.parameters
documentation: An object to hold reusable [Parameter Objects](#parameterObject).

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.components.Components.examples
documentation: An object to hold reusable [Example Objects](#exampleObject).

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.components.Components.requestBodies
documentation: An object to hold reusable [Request Body Objects](#requestBodyObject).

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.components.Components.headers
documentation: An object to hold reusable [Header Objects](#headerObject).

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.components.Components.securitySchemes
documentation: An object to hold reusable [Security Scheme Objects](#securitySchemeObject).

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.components.Components.links
documentation: An object to hold reusable [Link Objects](#linkObject).

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.components.Components.callbacks
documentation: An object to hold reusable [Callback Objects](#callbackObject).

Holds a set of reusable objects for different aspects of the OAS.
All objects defined within the components object will have no effect on the API
unless they are explicitly referenced from properties outside the components object.

classes.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.tag.Tag

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.tag.Tag.name
documentation: **REQUIRED**. The name of the tag.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.tag.Tag.description
documentation:
A short description for the tag.
[CommonMark syntax](https://spec.commonmark.org/) MAY be used for rich text representation.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.tag.Tag.externalDocs
documentation: Additional external documentation for this tag.

Adds metadata to a single tag that is used by the [Operation Object](#operationObject).
It is not mandatory to have a Tag Object per tag defined in the Operation Object instances.

classes.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.path_item.PathItem

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.path_item.PathItem.ref
documentation:
Allows for an external definition of this path item.
The referenced structure MUST be in the format of a [Path Item Object](#pathItemObject).

In case a Path Item Object field appears both in the defined object and the referenced object,
the behavior is undefined.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.path_item.PathItem.summary
documentation: An optional, string summary, intended to apply to all operations in this path.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.path_item.PathItem.description
documentation:
An optional, string description, intended to apply to all operations in this path.
[CommonMark syntax](https://spec.commonmark.org/) MAY be used for rich text representation.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.path_item.PathItem.get
documentation: A definition of a GET operation on this path.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.path_item.PathItem.put
documentation: A definition of a PUT operation on this path.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.path_item.PathItem.post
documentation: A definition of a POST operation on this path.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.path_item.PathItem.delete
documentation: A definition of a DELETE operation on this path.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.path_item.PathItem.options
documentation: A definition of a OPTIONS operation on this path.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.path_item.PathItem.head
documentation: A definition of a HEAD operation on this path.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.path_item.PathItem.patch
documentation: A definition of a PATCH operation on this path.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.path_item.PathItem.trace
documentation: A definition of a TRACE operation on this path.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.path_item.PathItem.servers
documentation: An alternative `server` array to service all operations in this path.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.path_item.PathItem.parameters
documentation:
A list of parameters that are applicable for all the operations described under this path.
These parameters can be overridden at the operation level, but cannot be removed there.
The list MUST NOT include duplicated parameters.
A unique parameter is defined by a combination of a [name](#parameterName) and [location](#parameterIn).
The list can use the [Reference Object](#referenceObject) to link to parameters that are defined at the
[OpenAPI Object's components/parameters](#componentsParameters).

Describes the operations available on a single path.
A Path Item MAY be empty, due to [ACL constraints](#securityFiltering).
The path itself is still exposed to the documentation viewer
but they will not know which operations and parameters are available.

classes.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.link.Link

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.link.Link.operationRef
documentation:
A relative or absolute URI reference to an OAS operation.
This field is mutually exclusive of the `operationId` field,
and MUST point to an [Operation Object](#operationObject).
Relative `operationRef` values MAY be used to locate an existing [Operation Object](#operationObject)
in the OpenAPI definition.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.link.Link.operationId
documentation:
The name of an _existing_, resolvable OAS operation, as defined with a unique `operationId`.

This field is mutually exclusive of the `operationRef` field.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.link.Link.parameters
documentation:
A map representing parameters to pass to an operation
as specified with `operationId` or identified via `operationRef`.
The key is the parameter name to be used,
whereas the value can be a constant or an expression to be evaluated and passed to the linked operation.

The parameter name can be qualified using the [parameter location](#parameterIn) `[{in}.]{name}`
for operations that use the same parameter name in different locations (e.g. path.id).

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.link.Link.requestBody
documentation: A literal value or [{expression}](#runtimeExpression) to use as a request body when calling the target operation.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.link.Link.description
documentation:
A description of the link.
[CommonMark syntax](https://spec.commonmark.org/) MAY be used for rich text representation.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.link.Link.server
documentation: A server object to be used by the target operation.

The `Link object` represents a possible design-time link for a response.
The presence of a link does not guarantee the caller's ability to successfully invoke it,
rather it provides a known relationship and traversal mechanism between responses and other operations.

Unlike _dynamic_ links (i.e. links provided **in** the response payload),
the OAS linking mechanism does not require link information in the runtime response.

For computing links, and providing instructions to execute them,
a [runtime expression](#runtimeExpression) is used for accessing values in an operation
and using them as parameters while invoking the linked operation.

classes.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.oauth_flow.OAuthFlow

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.oauth_flow.OAuthFlow.authorizationUrl
documentation:
**REQUIRED** for `oauth2 ("implicit", "authorizationCode")`.
The authorization URL to be used for this flow.
This MUST be in the form of a URL.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.oauth_flow.OAuthFlow.tokenUrl
documentation:
**REQUIRED** for `oauth2 ("password", "clientCredentials", "authorizationCode")`.
The token URL to be used for this flow.
This MUST be in the form of a URL.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.oauth_flow.OAuthFlow.refreshUrl
documentation: The URL to be used for obtaining refresh tokens. This MUST be in the form of a URL.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.oauth_flow.OAuthFlow.scopes
documentation:
**REQUIRED**. The available scopes for the OAuth2 security scheme.
A map between the scope name and a short description for it.
The map MAY be empty.
Configuration details for a supported OAuth Flow

modules.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3
documentation:
OpenAPI v3.0.3 schema types, created according to the specification:
https://github.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification/blob/master/versions/3.0.3.md

The type orders are according to the contents of the specification:
https://github.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification/blob/master/versions/3.0.3.md#table-of-contents

classes.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.security_scheme.SecurityScheme

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.security_scheme.SecurityScheme.type
documentation:
**REQUIRED**. The type of the security scheme.
Valid values are `"apiKey"`, `"http"`, `"oauth2"`, `"openIdConnect"`.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.security_scheme.SecurityScheme.description
documentation:
A short description for security scheme.
[CommonMark syntax](https://spec.commonmark.org/) MAY be used for rich text representation.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.security_scheme.SecurityScheme.name
documentation: **REQUIRED** for `apiKey`. The name of the header, query or cookie parameter to be used.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.security_scheme.SecurityScheme.security_scheme_in
documentation: **REQUIRED** for `apiKey`. The location of the API key. Valid values are `"query"`, `"header"` or `"cookie"`.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.security_scheme.SecurityScheme.scheme
documentation:
**REQUIRED** for `http`. The name of the HTTP Authorization scheme to be used in the
[Authorization header as defined in RFC7235](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-5.1).

The values used SHOULD be registered in the
[IANA Authentication Scheme registry](https://www.iana.org/assignments/http-authschemes/http-authschemes.xhtml).

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.security_scheme.SecurityScheme.bearerFormat
documentation:
A hint to the client to identify how the bearer token is formatted.

Bearer tokens are usually generated by an authorization server,
so this information is primarily for documentation purposes.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.security_scheme.SecurityScheme.flows
documentation: **REQUIRED** for `oauth2`. An object containing configuration information for the flow types supported.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.security_scheme.SecurityScheme.openIdConnectUrl
documentation:
**REQUIRED** for `openIdConnect`. OpenId Connect URL to discover OAuth2 configuration values.
This MUST be in the form of a URL.

Defines a security scheme that can be used by the operations.
Supported schemes are HTTP authentication,
an API key (either as a header, a cookie parameter or as a query parameter),
OAuth2's common flows (implicit, password, client credentials and authorization code)
as defined in [RFC6749](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6749),
and [OpenID Connect Discovery](https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-ietf-oauth-discovery-06).

classes.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.license.License

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.license.License.name
documentation: **REQUIRED**. The license name used for the API.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.license.License.url
documentation:
A URL to the license used for the API.
MUST be in the format of a URL.
License information for the exposed API.

classes.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.header.Header
documentation:
The Header Object follows the structure of the [Parameter Object](#parameterObject) with the following changes:

1. `name` MUST NOT be specified, it is given in the corresponding `headers` map.
2. `in` MUST NOT be specified, it is implicitly in `header`.
3. All traits that are affected by the location MUST be applicable to a location of `header`
   (for example, [`style`](#parameterStyle)).

classes.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.external_documentation.ExternalDocumentation

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.external_documentation.ExternalDocumentation.description
documentation:
A short description of the target documentation.
[CommonMark syntax](https://spec.commonmark.org/) MAY be used for rich text representation.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.external_documentation.ExternalDocumentation.url
documentation:
**REQUIRED**. The URL for the target documentation.
Value MUST be in the format of a URL.
Allows referencing an external resource for extended documentation.

classes.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.reference.Reference

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.reference.Reference.ref
documentation: **REQUIRED**. The reference string.

A simple object to allow referencing other components in the specification, internally and externally.

The Reference Object is defined by [JSON Reference](https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-pbryan-zyp-json-ref-03)
and follows the same structure, behavior and rules.

For this specification, reference resolution is accomplished as defined by the JSON Reference specification
and not by the JSON Schema specification.

functions.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.util.construct_open_api_with_schema_class
documentation:
Construct a new OpenAPI object, with the use of pydantic classes to produce JSON schemas

```
param open_api

the base  object

param schema_classes

pydanitic classes that their schema will be used "#/components/schemas" values

param scan_for_pydantic_schema_reference

flag to indicate if scanning for  class
is needed for "#/components/schemas" value updates

param by_alias

construct schema by alias (default is True)

return

new OpenAPI object with "#/components/schemas" values updated.
If there is no update in "#/components/schemas" values, the original  will be returned.
```

functions.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.util._handle_pydantic_schema
documentation:
This function traverses the `OpenAPI` object and

1. Replaces the `PydanticSchema` object with `Reference` object, with correct ref value;
2. Extracts the involved schema class from `PydanticSchema` object.

**This function will mutate the input \`OpenAPI\` object.**

```
param open_api

the  object to be traversed and mutated

return

a list of schema classes extracted from  objects
```

classes.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.util.PydanticSchema

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.util.PydanticSchema.schema_class
documentation: the class that is used for generate the schema
Special `Schema` class to indicate a reference from pydantic class

classes.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.info.Info

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.info.Info.title
documentation: **REQUIRED**. The title of the API.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.info.Info.description
documentation:
A short description of the API.
[CommonMark syntax](https://spec.commonmark.org/) MAY be used for rich text representation.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.info.Info.termsOfService
documentation:
A URL to the Terms of Service for the API.
MUST be in the format of a URL.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.info.Info.contact
documentation: The contact information for the exposed API.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.info.Info.license
documentation: The license information for the exposed API.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.info.Info.version
documentation:
**REQUIRED**. The version of the OpenAPI document
(which is distinct from the [OpenAPI Specification version](#oasVersion) or the API implementation version).

The object provides metadata about the API.
The metadata MAY be used by the clients if needed,
and MAY be presented in editing or documentation generation tools for convenience.

classes.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.server_variable.ServerVariable

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.server_variable.ServerVariable.enum
documentation:
An enumeration of string values to be used if the substitution options are from a limited set.
The array SHOULD NOT be empty.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.server_variable.ServerVariable.default
documentation:
**REQUIRED**. The default value to use for substitution,
which SHALL be sent if an alternate value is _not_ supplied.
Note this behavior is different than the [Schema Object's](#schemaObject) treatment of default values, 
because in those cases parameter values are optional.
If the [`enum`](#serverVariableEnum) is defined, the value SHOULD exist in the enum's values.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.server_variable.ServerVariable.description
documentation:
An optional description for the server variable.
[CommonMark syntax](https://spec.commonmark.org/) MAY be used for rich text representation.
An object representing a Server Variable for server URL template substitution.

classes.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.encoding.Encoding

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.encoding.Encoding.contentType
openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.encoding.Encoding.headers
documentation:
A map allowing additional information to be provided as headers, for example `Content-Disposition`.

`Content-Type` is described separately and SHALL be ignored in this section.
This property SHALL be ignored if the request body media type is not a `multipart`.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.encoding.Encoding.style
documentation:
Describes how a specific property value will be serialized depending on its type.

See [Parameter Object](#parameterObject) for details on the [`style`](#parameterStyle) property.
The behavior follows the same values as `query` parameters, including default values.
This property SHALL be ignored if the request body media type is not `application/x-www-form-urlencoded`.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.encoding.Encoding.explode
documentation:
When this is true, property values of type `array` or `object` generate separate parameters
for each value of the array, or key-value-pair of the map.

For other types of properties this property has no effect.
When [`style`](#encodingStyle) is `form`, the default value is `true`.
For all other styles, the default value is `false`.
This property SHALL be ignored if the request body media type is not `application/x-www-form-urlencoded`.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.encoding.Encoding.allowReserved
documentation:
Determines whether the parameter value SHOULD allow reserved characters,
as defined by [RFC3986](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3986#section-2.2)
`:/?#[]@!$&'()*+,;=` to be included without percent-encoding.
The default value is `false`.
This property SHALL be ignored if the request body media type is not `application/x-www-form-urlencoded`.
A single encoding definition applied to a single schema property.

classes.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.discriminator.Discriminator

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.discriminator.Discriminator.propertyName
documentation: **REQUIRED**. The name of the property in the payload that will hold the discriminator value.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.discriminator.Discriminator.mapping
documentation: An object to hold mappings between payload values and schema names or references.

When request bodies or response payloads may be one of a number of different schemas,
a `discriminator` object can be used to aid in serialization, deserialization, and validation.

The discriminator is a specific object in a schema which is used to inform the consumer of the specification
of an alternative schema based on the value associated with it.

When using the discriminator, _inline_ schemas will not be considered.

classes.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.contact.Contact

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.contact.Contact.name
documentation: The identifying name of the contact person/organization.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.contact.Contact.url
documentation:
The URL pointing to the contact information.
MUST be in the format of a URL.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.contact.Contact.email
documentation:
The email address of the contact person/organization.
MUST be in the format of an email address.
Contact information for the exposed API.

variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.responses.Responses
documentation:
A container for the expected responses of an operation.
The container maps a HTTP response code to the expected response.

The documentation is not necessarily expected to cover all possible HTTP response codes
because they may not be known in advance.
However, documentation is expected to cover a successful operation response and any known errors.

The `default` MAY be used as a default response object for all HTTP codes
that are not covered individually by the specification.

The `Responses Object` MUST contain at least one response code, and it
SHOULD be the response for a successful operation call.

variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.paths.Paths
documentation:
Holds the relative paths to the individual endpoints and their operations.
The path is appended to the URL from the [`Server Object`](#serverObject) in order to construct the full URL.

The Paths MAY be empty, due to [ACL constraints](#securityFiltering).

variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.security_requirement.SecurityRequirement
documentation:
Lists the required security schemes to execute this operation.
The name used for each property MUST correspond to a security scheme declared in the
[Security Schemes](#componentsSecuritySchemes) under the [Components Object](#componentsObject).

Security Requirement Objects that contain multiple schemes require that
all schemes MUST be satisfied for a request to be authorized.
This enables support for scenarios where multiple query parameters or HTTP headers
are required to convey security information.

When a list of Security Requirement Objects is defined on the
[OpenAPI Object](#oasObject) or [Operation Object](#operationObject),
only one of the Security Requirement Objects in the list needs to be satisfied to authorize the request.

classes.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.schema.Schema

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.schema.Schema.title
documentation:
The value of "title" MUST be a string.

The title can be used to decorate a user interface with
information about the data produced by this user interface.
The title will preferrably be short.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.schema.Schema.multipleOf
documentation:
The value of "multipleOf" MUST be a number, strictly greater than 0.

A numeric instance is only valid if division by this keyword's value
results in an integer.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.schema.Schema.maximum
documentation:
The value of "maximum" MUST be a number, representing an upper limit
for a numeric instance.

If the instance is a number, then this keyword validates if
"exclusiveMaximum" is true and instance is less than the provided
value, or else if the instance is less than or exactly equal to the
provided value.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.schema.Schema.exclusiveMaximum
documentation:
The value of "exclusiveMaximum" MUST be a boolean, representing
whether the limit in "maximum" is exclusive or not.  An undefined
value is the same as false.

If "exclusiveMaximum" is true, then a numeric instance SHOULD NOT be
equal to the value specified in "maximum".  If "exclusiveMaximum" is
false (or not specified), then a numeric instance MAY be equal to the
value of "maximum".

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.schema.Schema.minimum
documentation:
The value of "minimum" MUST be a number, representing a lower limit
for a numeric instance.

If the instance is a number, then this keyword validates if
"exclusiveMinimum" is true and instance is greater than the provided
value, or else if the instance is greater than or exactly equal to
the provided value.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.schema.Schema.exclusiveMinimum
documentation:
The value of "exclusiveMinimum" MUST be a boolean, representing
whether the limit in "minimum" is exclusive or not.  An undefined
value is the same as false.

If "exclusiveMinimum" is true, then a numeric instance SHOULD NOT be
equal to the value specified in "minimum".  If "exclusiveMinimum" is
false (or not specified), then a numeric instance MAY be equal to the
value of "minimum".

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.schema.Schema.maxLength
documentation:
The value of this keyword MUST be a non-negative integer.

The value of this keyword MUST be an integer.  This integer MUST be
greater than, or equal to, 0.

A string instance is valid against this keyword if its length is less
than, or equal to, the value of this keyword.

The length of a string instance is defined as the number of its
characters as defined by RFC 7159 [RFC7159].

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.schema.Schema.minLength
documentation:
A string instance is valid against this keyword if its length is
greater than, or equal to, the value of this keyword.

The length of a string instance is defined as the number of its
characters as defined by RFC 7159 [RFC7159].

The value of this keyword MUST be an integer.  This integer MUST be
greater than, or equal to, 0.

"minLength", if absent, may be considered as being present with
integer value 0.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.schema.Schema.pattern
documentation:
The value of this keyword MUST be a string.  This string SHOULD be a
valid regular expression, according to the ECMA 262 regular
expression dialect.

A string instance is considered valid if the regular expression
matches the instance successfully.  Recall: regular expressions are
not implicitly anchored.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.schema.Schema.maxItems
documentation:
The value of this keyword MUST be an integer.  This integer MUST be
greater than, or equal to, 0.

An array instance is valid against "maxItems" if its size is less
than, or equal to, the value of this keyword.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.schema.Schema.minItems
documentation:
The value of this keyword MUST be an integer.  This integer MUST be
greater than, or equal to, 0.

An array instance is valid against "minItems" if its size is greater
than, or equal to, the value of this keyword.

If this keyword is not present, it may be considered present with a
value of 0.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.schema.Schema.uniqueItems
documentation:
The value of this keyword MUST be a boolean.

If this keyword has boolean value false, the instance validates
successfully.  If it has boolean value true, the instance validates
successfully if all of its elements are unique.

If not present, this keyword may be considered present with boolean
value false.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.schema.Schema.maxProperties
documentation:
The value of this keyword MUST be an integer.  This integer MUST be
greater than, or equal to, 0.

An object instance is valid against "maxProperties" if its number of
properties is less than, or equal to, the value of this keyword.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.schema.Schema.minProperties
documentation:
The value of this keyword MUST be an integer.  This integer MUST be
greater than, or equal to, 0.

An object instance is valid against "minProperties" if its number of
properties is greater than, or equal to, the value of this keyword.

If this keyword is not present, it may be considered present with a
value of 0.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.schema.Schema.required
documentation:
The value of this keyword MUST be an array.  This array MUST have at
least one element.  Elements of this array MUST be strings, and MUST
be unique.

An object instance is valid against this keyword if its property set
contains all elements in this keyword's array value.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.schema.Schema.enum
documentation:
The value of this keyword MUST be an array.  This array SHOULD have
at least one element.  Elements in the array SHOULD be unique.

Elements in the array MAY be of any type, including null.

An instance validates successfully against this keyword if its value
is equal to one of the elements in this keyword's array value.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.schema.Schema.type
documentation:
**From OpenAPI spec:
Value MUST be a string. Multiple types via an array are not supported.**

From JSON Schema:
The value of this keyword MUST be either a string or an array.  If it
is an array, elements of the array MUST be strings and MUST be
unique.

String values MUST be one of the seven primitive types defined by the
core specification.

An instance matches successfully if its primitive type is one of the
types defined by keyword.  Recall: "number" includes "integer".

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.schema.Schema.allOf
documentation:
**From OpenAPI spec:
Inline or referenced schema MUST be of a [Schema Object](#schemaObject) and not a standard JSON Schema.**

From JSON Schema:
This keyword's value MUST be an array.  This array MUST have at least
one element.

Elements of the array MUST be objects.  Each object MUST be a valid
JSON Schema.

An instance validates successfully against this keyword if it
validates successfully against all schemas defined by this keyword's
value.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.schema.Schema.oneOf
documentation:
**From OpenAPI spec:
Inline or referenced schema MUST be of a [Schema Object](#schemaObject) and not a standard JSON Schema.**

From JSON Schema:
This keyword's value MUST be an array.  This array MUST have at least
one element.

Elements of the array MUST be objects.  Each object MUST be a valid
JSON Schema.

An instance validates successfully against this keyword if it
validates successfully against exactly one schema defined by this
keyword's value.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.schema.Schema.anyOf
documentation:
**From OpenAPI spec:
Inline or referenced schema MUST be of a [Schema Object](#schemaObject) and not a standard JSON Schema.**

From JSON Schema:
This keyword's value MUST be an array.  This array MUST have at least
one element.

Elements of the array MUST be objects.  Each object MUST be a valid
JSON Schema.

An instance validates successfully against this keyword if it
validates successfully against at least one schema defined by this
keyword's value.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.schema.Schema.schema_not
documentation:
**From OpenAPI spec:
Inline or referenced schema MUST be of a [Schema Object](#schemaObject) and not a standard JSON Schema.**

From JSON Schema:
This keyword's value MUST be an object.  This object MUST be a valid
JSON Schema.

An instance is valid against this keyword if it fails to validate
successfully against the schema defined by this keyword.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.schema.Schema.items
documentation:
**From OpenAPI spec:
Value MUST be an object and not an array.
Inline or referenced schema MUST be of a [Schema Object](#schemaObject) and not a standard JSON Schema.
`items` MUST be present if the `type` is `array`.**

From JSON Schema:
The value of "items" MUST be either a schema or array of schemas.

Successful validation of an array instance with regards to these two
keywords is determined as follows:

- if "items" is not present, or its value is an object, validation
  of the instance always succeeds, regardless of the value of
  "additionalItems";
- if the value of "additionalItems" is boolean value true or an
  object, validation of the instance always succeeds;
- if the value of "additionalItems" is boolean value false and the
  value of "items" is an array, the instance is valid if its size is
  less than, or equal to, the size of "items".

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.schema.Schema.properties
documentation:
**From OpenAPI spec:
Property definitions MUST be a [Schema Object](#schemaObject)
and not a standard JSON Schema (inline or referenced).**

From JSON Schema:
The value of "properties" MUST be an object.  Each value of this
object MUST be an object, and each object MUST be a valid JSON
Schema.

If absent, it can be considered the same as an empty object.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.schema.Schema.additionalProperties
documentation:
**From OpenAPI spec:
Value can be boolean or object.
Inline or referenced schema MUST be of a [Schema Object](#schemaObject) and not a standard JSON Schema.
Consistent with JSON Schema, `additionalProperties` defaults to `true`.**

From JSON Schema:
The value of "additionalProperties" MUST be a boolean or a schema.

If "additionalProperties" is absent, it may be considered present
with an empty schema as a value.

If "additionalProperties" is true, validation always succeeds.

If "additionalProperties" is false, validation succeeds only if the
instance is an object and all properties on the instance were covered
by "properties" and/or "patternProperties".

If "additionalProperties" is an object, validate the value as a
schema to all of the properties that weren't validated by
"properties" nor "patternProperties".

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.schema.Schema.description
documentation:
**From OpenAPI spec:
[CommonMark syntax](https://spec.commonmark.org/) MAY be used for rich text representation.**

From JSON Schema:
The value "description" MUST be a string.

The description can be used to decorate a user interface with
information about the data produced by this user interface.
The description will provide explanation about the purpose of
the instance described by this schema.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.schema.Schema.schema_format
documentation:
**From OpenAPI spec:
[Data Type Formats](#dataTypeFormat) for further details.
While relying on JSON Schema's defined formats, the OAS offers a few additional predefined formats.**

From JSON Schema:
Structural validation alone may be insufficient to validate that an
instance meets all the requirements of an application.  The "format"
keyword is defined to allow interoperable semantic validation for a
fixed subset of values which are accurately described by
authoritative resources, be they RFCs or other external
specifications.

The value of this keyword is called a format attribute.  It MUST be a
string.  A format attribute can generally only validate a given set
of instance types.  If the type of the instance to validate is not in
this set, validation for this format attribute and instance SHOULD
succeed.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.schema.Schema.default
documentation:
**From OpenAPI spec:
The default value represents what would be assumed by the consumer of the input
as the value of the schema if one is not provided.
Unlike JSON Schema, the value MUST conform to the defined type for the Schema Object defined at the same level.
For example, if `type` is `string`, then `default` can be `"foo"` but cannot be `1`.**

From JSON Schema:
There are no restrictions placed on the value of this keyword.

This keyword can be used to supply a default JSON value associated
with a particular schema.  It is RECOMMENDED that a default value be
valid against the associated schema.

This keyword MAY be used in root schemas, and in any subschemas.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.schema.Schema.nullable
documentation:
A `true` value adds `"null"` to the allowed type specified by the `type` keyword,
only if `type` is explicitly defined within the same Schema Object.
Other Schema Object constraints retain their defined behavior,
and therefore may disallow the use of `null` as a value.
A `false` value leaves the specified or default `type` unmodified.
The default value is `false`.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.schema.Schema.discriminator
documentation:
Adds support for polymorphism.
The discriminator is an object name that is used to differentiate between other schemas
which may satisfy the payload description.
See [Composition and Inheritance](#schemaComposition) for more details.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.schema.Schema.readOnly
documentation:
Relevant only for Schema `"properties"` definitions.
Declares the property as "read only".
This means that it MAY be sent as part of a response but SHOULD NOT be sent as part of the request.
If the property is marked as `readOnly` being `true` and is in the `required` list,
the `required` will take effect on the response only.
A property MUST NOT be marked as both `readOnly` and `writeOnly` being `true`.
Default value is `false`.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.schema.Schema.writeOnly
documentation:
Relevant only for Schema `"properties"` definitions.
Declares the property as "write only".
Therefore, it MAY be sent as part of a request but SHOULD NOT be sent as part of the response.
If the property is marked as `writeOnly` being `true` and is in the `required` list,
the `required` will take effect on the request only.
A property MUST NOT be marked as both `readOnly` and `writeOnly` being `true`.
Default value is `false`.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.schema.Schema.xml
documentation:
This MAY be used only on properties schemas.
It has no effect on root schemas.
Adds additional metadata to describe the XML representation of this property.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.schema.Schema.externalDocs
documentation: Additional external documentation for this schema.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.schema.Schema.example
documentation:
A free-form property to include an example of an instance for this schema.
To represent examples that cannot be naturally represented in JSON or YAML,
a string value can be used to contain the example with escaping where necessary.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.schema.Schema.deprecated
documentation:
Specifies that a schema is deprecated and SHOULD be transitioned out of usage.
Default value is `false`.

The Schema Object allows the definition of input and output data types.
These types can be objects, but also primitives and arrays.
This object is an extended subset of the [JSON Schema Specification Wright Draft 00](https://json-schema.org/).

For more information about the properties,
see [JSON Schema Core](https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-wright-json-schema-00)
and [JSON Schema Validation](https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-wright-json-schema-validation-00).
Unless stated otherwise, the property definitions follow the JSON Schema.

variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.callback.Callback
documentation:
A map of possible out-of band callbacks related to the parent operation.
Each value in the map is a [Path Item Object](#pathItemObject)
that describes a set of requests that may be initiated by the API provider and the expected responses.
The key value used to identify the path item object is an expression, evaluated at runtime,
that identifies a URL to use for the callback operation.

classes.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.response.Response

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.response.Response.description
documentation:
**REQUIRED**. A short description of the response.
[CommonMark syntax](https://spec.commonmark.org/) MAY be used for rich text representation.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.response.Response.headers
documentation:
Maps a header name to its definition.
[RFC7230](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7230#page-22) states header names are case insensitive.
If a response header is defined with the name `"Content-Type"`, it SHALL be ignored.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.response.Response.content
documentation:
A map containing descriptions of potential response payloads.
The key is a media type or [media type range](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#appendix-D)
and the value describes it.  

For responses that match multiple keys, only the most specific key is applicable. e.g. text/plain overrides text/*

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.response.Response.links
documentation:
A map of operations links that can be followed from the response.
The key of the map is a short name for the link,
following the naming constraints of the names for [Component Objects](#componentsObject).

Describes a single response from an API Operation, including design-time,
static `links` to operations based on the response.

classes.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.oauth_flows.OAuthFlows

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.oauth_flows.OAuthFlows.implicit
documentation: Configuration for the OAuth Implicit flow

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.oauth_flows.OAuthFlows.password
documentation: Configuration for the OAuth Resource Owner Password flow

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.oauth_flows.OAuthFlows.clientCredentials
documentation:
Configuration for the OAuth Client Credentials flow.

Previously called `application` in OpenAPI 2.0.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.oauth_flows.OAuthFlows.authorizationCode
documentation:
Configuration for the OAuth Authorization Code flow.

Previously called `accessCode` in OpenAPI 2.0.
Allows configuration of the supported OAuth Flows.

classes.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.media_type.MediaType

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.media_type.MediaType.media_type_schema
documentation: The schema defining the content of the request, response, or parameter.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.media_type.MediaType.example
documentation:
Example of the media type.

The example object SHOULD be in the correct format as specified by the media type.

The `example` field is mutually exclusive of the `examples` field.

Furthermore, if referencing a `schema` which contains an example,
the `example` value SHALL _override_ the example provided by the schema.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.media_type.MediaType.examples
documentation:
Examples of the media type.

Each example object SHOULD match the media type and specified schema if present.

The `examples` field is mutually exclusive of the `example` field.

Furthermore, if referencing a `schema` which contains an example,
the `examples` value SHALL _override_ the example provided by the schema.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.media_type.MediaType.encoding
documentation:
A map between a property name and its encoding information.
The key, being the property name, MUST exist in the schema as a property.
The encoding object SHALL only apply to `requestBody` objects
when the media type is `multipart` or `application/x-www-form-urlencoded`.
Each Media Type Object provides schema and examples for the media type identified by its key.

classes.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.parameter.Parameter

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.parameter.Parameter.name
documentation:
**REQUIRED**. The name of the parameter.
Parameter names are *case sensitive*. 

- If [`in`](#parameterIn) is `"path"`, the `name` field MUST correspond to a template expression occurring 
  within the [path](#pathsPath) field in the [Paths Object](#pathsObject).
  See [Path Templating](#pathTemplating) for further information.
- If [`in`](#parameterIn) is `"header"` and the `name` field is `"Accept"`, `"Content-Type"` or `"Authorization"`, 
  the parameter definition SHALL be ignored.
- For all other cases, the `name` corresponds to the parameter name used by the [`in`](#parameterIn) property.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.parameter.Parameter.param_in
documentation: **REQUIRED**. The location of the parameter. Possible values are `"query"`, `"header"`, `"path"` or `"cookie"`.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.parameter.Parameter.description
documentation:
A brief description of the parameter.
This could contain examples of use.
[CommonMark syntax](https://spec.commonmark.org/) MAY be used for rich text representation.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.parameter.Parameter.required
documentation:
Determines whether this parameter is mandatory.
If the [parameter location](#parameterIn) is `"path"`, this property is **REQUIRED** and its value MUST be `true`.
Otherwise, the property MAY be included and its default value is `false`.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.parameter.Parameter.deprecated
documentation:
Specifies that a parameter is deprecated and SHOULD be transitioned out of usage.
Default value is `false`.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.parameter.Parameter.allowEmptyValue
documentation:
Sets the ability to pass empty-valued parameters.
This is valid only for `query` parameters and allows sending a parameter with an empty value.
Default value is `false`.
If [`style`](#parameterStyle) is used, and if behavior is `n/a` (cannot be serialized),
the value of `allowEmptyValue` SHALL be ignored.
Use of this property is NOT RECOMMENDED, as it is likely to be removed in a later revision.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.parameter.Parameter.style
openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.parameter.Parameter.explode
documentation:
When this is true, parameter values of type `array` or `object` generate separate parameters
for each value of the array or key-value pair of the map.
For other types of parameters this property has no effect.
When [`style`](#parameterStyle) is `form`, the default value is `true`.
For all other styles, the default value is `false`.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.parameter.Parameter.allowReserved
documentation:
Determines whether the parameter value SHOULD allow reserved characters,
as defined by [RFC3986](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3986#section-2.2)
`:/?#[]@!$&'()*+,;=` to be included without percent-encoding.
This property only applies to parameters with an `in` value of `query`.
The default value is `false`.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.parameter.Parameter.param_schema
documentation: The schema defining the type used for the parameter.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.parameter.Parameter.example
documentation:
Example of the parameter's potential value.
The example SHOULD match the specified schema and encoding properties if present.
The `example` field is mutually exclusive of the `examples` field.
Furthermore, if referencing a `schema` that contains an example, 
the `example` value SHALL _override_ the example provided by the schema.
To represent examples of media types that cannot naturally be represented in JSON or YAML,
a string value can contain the example with escaping where necessary.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.parameter.Parameter.examples
documentation:
Examples of the parameter's potential value.
Each example SHOULD contain a value in the correct format as specified in the parameter encoding.
The `examples` field is mutually exclusive of the `example` field.
Furthermore, if referencing a `schema` that contains an example,
the `examples` value SHALL _override_ the example provided by the schema.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_0_3.parameter.Parameter.content
documentation:
A map containing the representations for the parameter.
The key is the media type and the value describes it.
The map MUST only contain one entry.

Describes a single operation parameter.

A unique parameter is defined by a combination of a [name](#parameterName) and [location](#parameterIn).

classes.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.server.Server

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.server.Server.url
documentation:
**REQUIRED**. A URL to the target host.

This URL supports Server Variables and MAY be relative,
to indicate that the host location is relative to the location where the OpenAPI document is being served.
Variable substitutions will be made when a variable is named in `{`brackets`}`.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.server.Server.description
documentation:
An optional string describing the host designated by the URL.
[CommonMark syntax](https://spec.commonmark.org/) MAY be used for rich text representation.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.server.Server.variables
documentation:
A map between a variable name and its value.

The value is used for substitution in the server's URL template.
An object representing a Server.

classes.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.xml.XML

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.xml.XML.name
documentation:
Replaces the name of the element/attribute used for the described schema property.
When defined within `items`, it will affect the name of the individual XML elements within the list.
When defined alongside `type` being `array` (outside the `items`),
it will affect the wrapping element and only if `wrapped` is `true`.
If `wrapped` is `false`, it will be ignored.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.xml.XML.namespace
documentation:
The URI of the namespace definition.
Value MUST be in the form of an absolute URI.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.xml.XML.prefix
documentation: The prefix to be used for the [name](#xmlName).

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.xml.XML.attribute
documentation:
Declares whether the property definition translates to an attribute instead of an element.
Default value is `false`.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.xml.XML.wrapped
documentation:
MAY be used only for an array definition.
Signifies whether the array is wrapped (for example, `<books><book/><book/></books>`)
or unwrapped (`<book/><book/>`).
Default value is `false`.
The definition takes effect only when defined alongside `type` being `array` (outside the `items`).

A metadata object that allows for more fine-tuned XML model definitions.

When using arrays, XML element names are *not* inferred (for singular/plural forms)
and the `name` property SHOULD be used to add that information.
See examples for expected behavior.

classes.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.request_body.RequestBody

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.request_body.RequestBody.description
documentation:
A brief description of the request body.
This could contain examples of use.  

[CommonMark syntax](https://spec.commonmark.org/) MAY be used for rich text representation.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.request_body.RequestBody.content
documentation:
**REQUIRED**. The content of the request body.
The key is a media type or [media type range](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#appendix-D)
and the value describes it.

For requests that match multiple keys, only the most specific key is applicable. e.g. text/plain overrides text/*

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.request_body.RequestBody.required
documentation: Determines if the request body is required in the request. Defaults to `false`.
Describes a single request body.

classes.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.operation.Operation

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.operation.Operation.tags
documentation:
A list of tags for API documentation control.
Tags can be used for logical grouping of operations by resources or any other qualifier.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.operation.Operation.summary
documentation: A short summary of what the operation does.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.operation.Operation.description
documentation:
A verbose explanation of the operation behavior.
[CommonMark syntax](https://spec.commonmark.org/) MAY be used for rich text representation.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.operation.Operation.externalDocs
documentation: Additional external documentation for this operation.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.operation.Operation.operationId
documentation:
Unique string used to identify the operation.
The id MUST be unique among all operations described in the API.
The operationId value is **case-sensitive**.
Tools and libraries MAY use the operationId to uniquely identify an operation,
therefore, it is RECOMMENDED to follow common programming naming conventions.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.operation.Operation.parameters
documentation:
A list of parameters that are applicable for this operation.
If a parameter is already defined at the [Path Item](#pathItemParameters),
the new definition will override it but can never remove it.
The list MUST NOT include duplicated parameters.
A unique parameter is defined by a combination of a [name](#parameterName) and [location](#parameterIn).
The list can use the [Reference Object](#referenceObject) to link to parameters
that are defined at the [OpenAPI Object's components/parameters](#componentsParameters).

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.operation.Operation.requestBody
documentation:
The request body applicable for this operation.  

The `requestBody` is fully supported in HTTP methods where the HTTP 1.1 specification
[RFC7231](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-4.3.1) has explicitly defined semantics for request bodies.
In other cases where the HTTP spec is vague (such as [GET](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-4.3.1),
[HEAD](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-4.3.2)
and [DELETE](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-4.3.5)),
`requestBody` is permitted but does not have well-defined semantics and SHOULD be avoided if possible.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.operation.Operation.responses
documentation: The list of possible responses as they are returned from executing this operation.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.operation.Operation.callbacks
documentation:
A map of possible out-of band callbacks related to the parent operation.
The key is a unique identifier for the Callback Object.
Each value in the map is a [Callback Object](#callbackObject) 
that describes a request that may be initiated by the API provider and the expected responses.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.operation.Operation.deprecated
documentation:
Declares this operation to be deprecated.
Consumers SHOULD refrain from usage of the declared operation.
Default value is `false`.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.operation.Operation.security
documentation:
A declaration of which security mechanisms can be used for this operation.
The list of values includes alternative security requirement objects that can be used.
Only one of the security requirement objects need to be satisfied to authorize a request.
To make security optional, an empty security requirement (`{}`) can be included in the array.
This definition overrides any declared top-level [`security`](#oasSecurity).
To remove a top-level security declaration, an empty array can be used.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.operation.Operation.servers
documentation:
An alternative `server` array to service this operation.
If an alternative `server` object is specified at the Path Item Object or Root level, 
it will be overridden by this value.
Describes a single API operation on a path.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.example.Example.summary
documentation: Short description for the example.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.example.Example.description
documentation:
Long description for the example.
[CommonMark syntax](https://spec.commonmark.org/) MAY be used for rich text representation.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.example.Example.value
documentation:
Embedded literal example.
The `value` field and `externalValue` field are mutually exclusive.
To represent examples of media types that cannot naturally represented in JSON or YAML,
use a string value to contain the example, escaping where necessary.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.example.Example.externalValue
documentation:
A URL that points to the literal example.
This provides the capability to reference examples that cannot easily be included in JSON or YAML documents.

The `value` field and `externalValue` field are mutually exclusive.
See the rules for resolving [Relative References](#relativeReferencesURI).

classes.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.components.Components

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.components.Components.schemas
documentation: An object to hold reusable [Schema Objects](#schemaObject).

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.components.Components.responses
documentation: An object to hold reusable [Response Objects](#responseObject).

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.components.Components.parameters
documentation: An object to hold reusable [Parameter Objects](#parameterObject).

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.components.Components.examples
documentation: An object to hold reusable [Example Objects](#exampleObject).

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.components.Components.requestBodies
documentation: An object to hold reusable [Request Body Objects](#requestBodyObject).

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.components.Components.headers
documentation: An object to hold reusable [Header Objects](#headerObject).

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.components.Components.securitySchemes
documentation: An object to hold reusable [Security Scheme Objects](#securitySchemeObject).

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.components.Components.links
documentation: An object to hold reusable [Link Objects](#linkObject).

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.components.Components.callbacks
documentation: An object to hold reusable [Callback Objects](#callbackObject).

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.components.Components.pathItems
documentation: An object to hold reusable [Path Item Object](#pathItemObject).

Holds a set of reusable objects for different aspects of the OAS.
All objects defined within the components object will have no effect on the API
unless they are explicitly referenced from properties outside the components object.

classes.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.open_api.OpenAPI

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.open_api.OpenAPI.openapi
documentation:
**REQUIRED**. This string MUST be the [version number](#versions)
of the OpenAPI Specification that the OpenAPI document uses.
The `openapi` field SHOULD be used by tooling to interpret the OpenAPI document.
This is *not* related to the API [`info.version`](#infoVersion) string.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.open_api.OpenAPI.info
documentation: **REQUIRED**. Provides metadata about the API. The metadata MAY be used by tooling as required.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.open_api.OpenAPI.jsonSchemaDialect
documentation:
The default value for the `$schema` keyword within [Schema Objects](#schemaObject)
contained within this OAS document. This MUST be in the form of a URI.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.open_api.OpenAPI.servers
documentation:
An array of Server Objects, which provide connectivity information to a target server. 
If the `servers` property is not provided, or is an empty array,
the default value would be a [Server Object](#serverObject) with a [url](#serverUrl) value of `/`.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.open_api.OpenAPI.paths
documentation: The available paths and operations for the API.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.open_api.OpenAPI.webhooks
documentation:
The incoming webhooks that MAY be received as part of this API and that the API consumer MAY choose to implement.
Closely related to the `callbacks` feature, this section describes requests initiated other than by an API call,
for example by an out of band registration.
The key name is a unique string to refer to each webhook,
while the (optionally referenced) Path Item Object describes a request
that may be initiated by the API provider and the expected responses.
An [example](../examples/v3.1/webhook-example.yaml) is available.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.open_api.OpenAPI.components
documentation: An element to hold various schemas for the document.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.open_api.OpenAPI.security
documentation:
A declaration of which security mechanisms can be used across the API. 
The list of values includes alternative security requirement objects that can be used. 
Only one of the security requirement objects need to be satisfied to authorize a request.  
Individual operations can override this definition. 
To make security optional, an empty security requirement (`{}`) can be included in the array.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.open_api.OpenAPI.tags
documentation:
A list of tags used by the document with additional metadata.
The order of the tags can be used to reflect on their order by the parsing tools.
Not all tags that are used by the [Operation Object](#operationObject) must be declared.
The tags that are not declared MAY be organized randomly or based on the tools' logic.
Each tag name in the list MUST be unique.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.open_api.OpenAPI.externalDocs
documentation: Additional external documentation.
This is the root document object of the OpenAPI document.

classes.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.tag.Tag

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.tag.Tag.name
documentation: **REQUIRED**. The name of the tag.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.tag.Tag.description
documentation:
A short description for the tag.
[CommonMark syntax](https://spec.commonmark.org/) MAY be used for rich text representation.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.tag.Tag.externalDocs
documentation: Additional external documentation for this tag.

Adds metadata to a single tag that is used by the [Operation Object](#operationObject).
It is not mandatory to have a Tag Object per tag defined in the Operation Object instances.

classes.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.path_item.PathItem

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.path_item.PathItem.ref
documentation:
Allows for an external definition of this path item.
The referenced structure MUST be in the format of a [Path Item Object](#pathItemObject).

In case a Path Item Object field appears both in the defined object and the referenced object,
the behavior is undefined.
See the rules for resolving [Relative References](#relativeReferencesURI).

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.path_item.PathItem.summary
documentation: An optional, string summary, intended to apply to all operations in this path.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.path_item.PathItem.description
documentation:
An optional, string description, intended to apply to all operations in this path.
[CommonMark syntax](https://spec.commonmark.org/) MAY be used for rich text representation.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.path_item.PathItem.get
documentation: A definition of a GET operation on this path.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.path_item.PathItem.put
documentation: A definition of a PUT operation on this path.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.path_item.PathItem.post
documentation: A definition of a POST operation on this path.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.path_item.PathItem.delete
documentation: A definition of a DELETE operation on this path.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.path_item.PathItem.options
documentation: A definition of a OPTIONS operation on this path.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.path_item.PathItem.head
documentation: A definition of a HEAD operation on this path.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.path_item.PathItem.patch
documentation: A definition of a PATCH operation on this path.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.path_item.PathItem.trace
documentation: A definition of a TRACE operation on this path.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.path_item.PathItem.servers
documentation: An alternative `server` array to service all operations in this path.

class_variables.name: openapi_schema_pydantic.v3.v3_1_0.path_item.PathItem.parameters
documentation:
A list of parameters that are applicable for all the operations described under this path.
These parameters can be overridden at the operation level, but cannot be removed there.
The list MUST NOT include duplicated parameters.
